description: ID of the account.
Statement id of query that inserted result in cache when result is fetched from cache, otherwise store the query's statement id.
Client application that ran the statement. For example: Databricks SQL, Tableau, and Power BI.
The connector used to connect to Databricks to run the statement. For example: Databricks SQL Driver for Go, Databricks ODBC Driver, Databricks JDBC Driver.
Time spent loading metadata and optimizing the statement in milliseconds.
A struct that represents the type of compute resource used to run the statement and the ID of the resource where applicable. The type value will be WAREHOUSE.
The time the statement execution ended, excluding result fetch time. Timezone information is recorded at the end of the value with +00:00 representing UTC.
Message describing the error condition. If you have configured customer-managed keys, error_message is empty.
The name of the user or service principal whose privilege was used to run the statement.
The ID of the user or service principal whose privilege was used to run the statement.
The email address or username of the user who ran the statement.
The ID of the user who ran the statement.
Time spent executing the statement in milliseconds.

The statement termination state. Possible values are:

FINISHED: execution was successful

FAILED: execution failed with the reason for failure described in the accompanying error message

CANCELED: execution was canceled
TRUE indicates that the statement result was fetched from the cache.
Total number of rows returned by the statement.
The number of pruned files during table partition and file pruning.
The number of bytes of files pruned after table partition and file pruning.
Query parameters values associated with the query. Only one of named_parameters or pos_parameters can be populated.

A struct that contains key-value pairs representing one or more Databricks entities that were involved in the execution of this statement, such as jobs, notebooks, or dashboards. This field only records Databricks entities and are not sorted by execution order. Statement executions that contain multiple IDs indicate that the execution was triggered by multiple entities: for example, an Alert may trigger on a Job result and call a SQL Query, so all three IDs will be populated within query_source.
The user-supplied custom tags associated with this statement execution.
The number of bytes of files read after IO pruning.
The number of files read after table partition and file pruning.
The number of bytes of files read after table partition and file pruning.
The percentage of bytes of persistent data read from the IO cache.
The number of table partitions read after table partition pruning.
The number of rows output from scans after table partition, file, in file and row pruning.
Time spent, in milliseconds, fetching the statement results after the execution finished.
The Spark session ID.
The total amount of data in bytes sent over the network.
Size of data, in bytes, temporarily written to disk while executing the statement.
The time when Databricks received the request. Timezone information is recorded at the end of the value with +00:00 representing UTC.
The ID that uniquely identifies the execution of the statement. You can use this ID to find the statement execution in the Query History UI.
Text of the SQL statement. If you have configured customer-managed keys, statement_text is empty.
The statement type. For example: ALTER, COPY, and`INSERT`.
Total execution time of the statement in milliseconds ( excluding result fetch time ).

The sum of all task durations in milliseconds. This time represents the combined time it took to run the query across all cores of all nodes. It can be significantly longer than the wall-clock duration if multiple tasks are executed in parallel. It can be shorter than the wall-clock duration if tasks wait for available nodes.
The time the statement last received a progress update. Timezone information is recorded at the end of the value with +00:00 representing UTC.
Time spent waiting in queue for available compute capacity in milliseconds.
Time spent waiting for compute resources to be provisioned in milliseconds.
The ID of the workspace where the query was run.
The size in bytes of persistent data written to cloud object storage.
The number of files of persistent data written to cloud object storage.
Total number of rows of persistent data written to cloud object storage.

Raw statement-level execution history for SQL workloads — duration, status, phases, bytes, rows, and the query_source struct that identifies the originating object (dashboard, Genie space, notebook, job, alert, pipeline). The base table for all performance and origin questions. Large — always filter on start_time.

Current state of every Genie space in the workspace. Join on space_id = query_source.genie_space_id to resolve space titles, see the configured warehouse, and surface metadata like instruction_count, table_count, sample_question_count, curated_question_count.

Current state of every Lakeview dashboard, enriched with 30-day view counts (from system.access.audit) and 30-day query stats (from system.query.history). Join on dashboard_id = query_source.dashboard_id to resolve dashboard names and surface unused dashboards.
Current state of every SQL warehouse — size, type, serverless flag, auto-stop, channel, creator. Join on id = compute.warehouse_id to resolve warehouse names and surface configuration alongside cost.
DBU usage records by workspace, warehouse, and SKU. The base table for raw warehouse spend. Join to account_prices (preferred) or list_prices to convert DBUs into USD.
Public catalog prices per SKU. Used as a fallback when account_prices does not have a row for a given (cloud, sku, price window).

Per-statement attributed warehouse cost (USD + DBU) using hourly proportional attribution. Key columns: statement_id, executed_by, warehouse_id, dashboard_id, genie_space_id, notebook_id, alert_id, job_id, client_application, work_ms, attributed_cost_usd, attributed_dbus. Use for cost-per-query, cost-per-user, and any custom rollup not already pre-built.

Per-dashboard attributed cost rollup. Window is configurable (default 365 days). Columns: dashboard_id, dashboard_name, owner, configured_warehouse_id, statements, cost_usd, dbus, avg_cost_per_statement_usd, last_statement_at. Use for "which dashboards cost the most" questions.

Per-Genie-space attributed cost rollup. Window is configurable (default 365 days). Columns: space_id, genie_space_title, configured_warehouse_id, creator_id, statements, cost_usd, dbus, avg_cost_per_statement_usd, unique_users, last_statement_at. Use for "which Genie spaces cost the most" questions.

Per-warehouse attributed cost rollup. Window is configurable (default 365 days). Columns: warehouse_id, warehouse_name, cluster_size, warehouse_type, enable_serverless_compute, statements, cost_usd, dbus, unique_users, dashboards_touched, genie_spaces_touched, last_statement_at. Use for "which warehouses cost the most / who touched them" questions.
The account's contracted prices per SKU. Prefer over list_prices for any cost calculation. Falls back to list_prices for (cloud, sku, price window) rows the account table doesn't cover.

content:
=== MISSION ===
This space answers three kinds of questions about Databricks SQL workloads:
  1. COST — "how much did dashboard / Genie space / user X cost", "cost trend", "$ per query"
  2. PERFORMANCE — "what's slow / queued / spilling / failing", percentile latencies, scan efficiency
  3. USAGE & INVENTORY — "which dashboards are actually used", "which Genie spaces have the most users", "what tools are people querying with"

=== WHERE TO LOOK (PICK THE RIGHT TABLE) ===
For COST questions, prefer the pre-built rollup tables. They already implement the hourly-bucket proportional attribution and use the customer's contracted rates where available:
  - per-dashboard cost          -> lakeview_dashboard_cost
  - per-Genie-space cost        -> genie_space_cost
  - per-warehouse cost          -> warehouse_cost
  - per-statement / per-user $  -> query_cost_attribution
Do NOT use total_duration_ms as a cost proxy. We have real dollars.

For PERFORMANCE and USAGE questions, use system.query.history directly. Join to the inventory tables for human-readable names.

For RAW BILLING (if explicitly asked for warehouse spend independent of attribution):
  system.billing.usage  JOIN  system.billing.account_prices  (fall back to list_prices)
  ON cloud + sku_name where usage_start_time BETWEEN price_start_time AND price_end_time
  cost = usage_quantity * pricing.default

=== TIME FILTERING (REQUIRED) ===
Always filter on start_time. system.query.history is very large.
If the user doesn't say, default to the last 7 days:
  start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
For COST rollups, default to the full window the tables already cover (no time filter needed unless the user asks).

=== QUERY SOURCE ATTRIBUTION ===
client_application = which TOOL connected (Tableau, Power BI, dbt, Databricks SQL Editor).
query_source        = which DATABRICKS ENTITY triggered the query.

Use this CASE WHEN to classify origin:
  CASE
    WHEN query_source.notebook_id IS NOT NULL                THEN 'Notebook'
    WHEN query_source.dashboard_id IS NOT NULL               THEN 'AI/BI Dashboard'
    WHEN query_source.legacy_dashboard_id IS NOT NULL        THEN 'Legacy Dashboard'
    WHEN query_source.job_info.job_id IS NOT NULL            THEN 'Job'
    WHEN query_source.alert_id IS NOT NULL                   THEN 'Alert'
    WHEN query_source.genie_space_id IS NOT NULL             THEN 'Genie Space'
    WHEN query_source.pipeline_info.pipeline_id IS NOT NULL  THEN 'DLT Pipeline'
    WHEN query_source.sql_query_id IS NOT NULL               THEN 'Saved SQL Query'
    ELSE 'Other / Ad-hoc'
  END AS query_source_type

A single query can have multiple source IDs populated (e.g. Job -> Notebook -> SQL Query). The struct is NOT sorted by execution order.

=== STRUCT COLUMNS — GOTCHAS ===
compute.warehouse_id, compute.type, compute.cluster_id  — flat
query_source.{dashboard_id, genie_space_id, notebook_id, alert_id, sql_query_id, legacy_dashboard_id}  — flat
query_source.job_info.{job_id, job_run_id, job_task_run_id}  — NESTED. job_id is NOT at query_source.job_id.
query_source.pipeline_info.{pipeline_id, update_id}  — NESTED.

=== SAFE MATH ===
Use try_divide() for any ratio or rate. Especially:
  - scan-to-output ratio (read_rows / produced_rows)
  - cache hit rate
  - any percentage

=== EXECUTION STATUS ===
execution_status in ('FINISHED', 'FAILED', 'CANCELED').
For performance and cost questions, filter to FINISHED unless the user is asking about errors.

=== PERFORMANCE RED FLAGS ===
- Slow queries: ORDER BY total_duration_ms DESC
- Queued: waiting_for_compute_duration_ms or waiting_at_capacity_duration_ms > 0
- Disk spill: spilled_local_bytes > 0 (ran out of memory)
- Poor scan efficiency: high read_rows / produced_rows ratio (e.g. 1000:1) suggests missing filters or weak pruning
- Failed: execution_status = 'FAILED' — check error_message

=== CACHING ===
from_result_cache = true  -> served from cache, zero compute cost
read_io_cache_percent     -> % of data served from IO cache
Use both for cache-hit analysis.

=== WORKSPACE INVENTORY TABLES (name resolution) ===
Always join these to show human-readable names instead of raw IDs.

lakeview_dashboard_denormalized:
  Join: dashboard_id = query_source.dashboard_id
  Useful columns: display_name, owner, path, lifecycle_state, schedule_count,
    total_task_duration_l30d, count_queries_l30d, views (struct of view counts).

genie_space_current:
  Join: space_id = query_source.genie_space_id
  Useful columns: title (space name), description, warehouse_id,
    table_count, instruction_count, sample_question_count, curated_question_count.

warehouse_current:
  Join: id = compute.warehouse_id
  Useful columns: name, state, warehouse_type, cluster_size,
    min_num_clusters, max_num_clusters, auto_stop_mins,
    enable_serverless_compute, creator_name.

question: Which Genie spaces generate the most queries in the last 7 days?
sql:
SELECT
  query_source.genie_space_id,
  COUNT(*) AS query_count,
  COUNT(DISTINCT executed_by) AS unique_users,
  ROUND(try_divide(AVG(total_duration_ms), 1000), 2) AS avg_duration_sec
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND query_source.genie_space_id IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY query_source.genie_space_id
ORDER BY query_count DESC
LIMIT 10

question: Show queries from Tableau in the last 7 days
sql:
SELECT
  executed_by,
  statement_text,
  try_divide(total_duration_ms, 1000) AS duration_sec,
  start_time
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND client_application ILIKE '%Tableau%'
ORDER BY start_time DESC
LIMIT 20

question: What is the daily query trend over the last 30 days?
sql:
SELECT
  DATE(start_time) AS query_date,
  COUNT(*) AS total_queries,
  COUNT(DISTINCT executed_by) AS unique_users,
  SUM(CASE WHEN execution_status = 'FAILED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS failed_queries,
  ROUND(AVG(total_duration_ms) / 1000, 2) AS avg_duration_sec
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 30 DAYS
GROUP BY DATE(start_time)
ORDER BY query_date

question: Query volume breakdown by source type (notebooks, dashboards, jobs, Genie, etc.)
sql:
SELECT
  CASE
    WHEN query_source.notebook_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Notebook'
    WHEN query_source.dashboard_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'AI/BI Dashboard'
    WHEN query_source.legacy_dashboard_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Legacy Dashboard'
    WHEN query_source.job_info.job_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Job'
    WHEN query_source.alert_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Alert'
    WHEN query_source.genie_space_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Genie Space'
    WHEN query_source.pipeline_info.pipeline_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'DLT Pipeline'
    WHEN query_source.sql_query_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Saved SQL Query'
    ELSE 'Other / Ad-hoc'
  END AS query_source_type,
  COUNT(*) AS query_count,
  COUNT(DISTINCT executed_by) AS unique_users,
  ROUND(try_divide(AVG(total_duration_ms), 1000), 2) AS avg_duration_sec
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
GROUP BY query_source_type
ORDER BY query_count DESC

question: Which jobs run the heaviest queries? Show top 10 by total data read.
sql:
SELECT
  query_source.job_info.job_id,
  COUNT(*) AS query_count,
  ROUND(try_divide(AVG(total_duration_ms), 1000), 2) AS avg_duration_sec,
  try_divide(SUM(read_bytes), (1024*1024*1024)) AS total_read_gb
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND query_source.job_info.job_id IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY query_source.job_info.job_id
ORDER BY total_read_gb DESC
LIMIT 10

question: Truncate long text for readable output
sql:
SELECT
  statement_text,
  error_message
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
LIMIT 10

question: Duration breakdown — where is time spent?
sql:
SELECT
  executed_by,
  statement_text,
  waiting_for_compute_duration_ms / 1000 AS compute_wait_sec,
  waiting_at_capacity_duration_ms / 1000 AS queue_wait_sec,
  compilation_duration_ms / 1000 AS compile_sec,
  execution_duration_ms / 1000 AS exec_sec,
  result_fetch_duration_ms / 1000 AS fetch_sec,
  total_duration_ms / 1000 AS total_sec
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND execution_status = 'FINISHED'
ORDER BY total_duration_ms DESC
LIMIT 10

question: Show queries from Power BI in the last 7 days
sql:
SELECT
  executed_by,
  statement_text,
  try_divide(total_duration_ms, 1000) AS duration_sec,
  start_time
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND client_application ILIKE '%Power BI%'
ORDER BY start_time DESC
LIMIT 20

question: What is the IO cache hit rate by warehouse?
sql:
SELECT
  compute.warehouse_id,
  COUNT(*) AS query_count,
  ROUND(AVG(read_io_cache_percent), 1) AS avg_io_cache_pct,
  ROUND(
    100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN from_result_cache THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*),
    1
  ) AS result_cache_hit_pct
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND execution_status = 'FINISHED'
GROUP BY compute.warehouse_id
ORDER BY query_count DESC

question: Which notebooks are the most active in the last 7 days?
sql:
SELECT
  query_source.notebook_id,
  COUNT(*) AS query_count,
  COUNT(DISTINCT executed_by) AS unique_users,
  ROUND(try_divide(AVG(total_duration_ms), 1000), 2) AS avg_duration_sec,
  try_divide(SUM(read_bytes), (1024*1024*1024)) AS total_read_gb
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND query_source.notebook_id IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY query_source.notebook_id
ORDER BY query_count DESC
LIMIT 10

question: Show queries from dbt in the last 7 days
sql:
SELECT
  executed_by,
  statement_text,
  try_divide(total_duration_ms, 1000) AS duration_sec,
  start_time
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND client_application ILIKE '%dbt%'
ORDER BY start_time DESC
LIMIT 20

question: Calculate percentage with window function
sql:
SELECT
  statement_type,
  COUNT(*) AS query_count,
  ROUND(
    100.0 * COUNT(*) / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER(),
    1
  ) AS pct_of_total
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
GROUP BY statement_type
ORDER BY query_count DESC

question: Show me the hourly query volume pattern for the last 7 days
sql:
SELECT
  HOUR(start_time) AS hour_of_day,
  COUNT(*) AS query_count,
  ROUND(AVG(total_duration_ms) / 1000, 2) AS avg_duration_sec,
  ROUND(MAX(total_duration_ms) / 1000, 2) AS max_duration_sec
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
GROUP BY HOUR(start_time)
ORDER BY hour_of_day

question: Safe division with try_divide to avoid divide-by-zero
sql:
SELECT
  try_divide(read_rows, produced_rows) AS scan_to_output_ratio,
  try_divide(
    SUM(CASE WHEN from_result_cache THEN 1 ELSE 0 END),
    COUNT(*)
  ) * 100 AS cache_hit_pct,
  try_divide(spilled_local_bytes, read_bytes) * 100 AS spill_pct
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
GROUP BY ALL
LIMIT 10

question: Convert bytes to GB
sql:
SELECT
  read_bytes / (1024*1024*1024) AS read_gb,
  written_bytes / (1024*1024*1024) AS written_gb,
  spilled_local_bytes / (1024*1024*1024) AS spill_gb,
  shuffle_read_bytes / (1024*1024*1024) AS shuffle_gb
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
LIMIT 10

question: Dashboard query volume — which dashboards drive the most queries?
sql:
SELECT
  COALESCE(query_source.dashboard_id, query_source.legacy_dashboard_id) AS dashboard_id,
  CASE
    WHEN query_source.dashboard_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'AI/BI'
    ELSE 'Legacy'
  END AS dashboard_type,
  COUNT(*) AS query_count,
  COUNT(DISTINCT executed_by) AS unique_viewers,
  ROUND(try_divide(AVG(total_duration_ms), 1000), 2) AS avg_duration_sec
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND (query_source.dashboard_id IS NOT NULL OR query_source.legacy_dashboard_id IS NOT NULL)
GROUP BY dashboard_id, dashboard_type
ORDER BY query_count DESC
LIMIT 10

question: Show me the top data writers — who is writing the most data?
sql:
SELECT
  executed_by,
  COUNT(*) AS write_queries,
  SUM(written_bytes) / (1024*1024*1024) AS written_gb,
  SUM(written_rows) AS total_rows_written
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND written_bytes > 0
GROUP BY executed_by
ORDER BY written_gb DESC
LIMIT 10

question: Show me the query breakdown by statement type
sql:
SELECT
  statement_type,
  COUNT(*) AS query_count,
  ROUND(
    100.0 * COUNT(*) / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER(),
    1
  ) AS pct,
  ROUND(AVG(total_duration_ms) / 1000, 2) AS avg_duration_sec
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
GROUP BY statement_type
ORDER BY query_count DESC

question: Which dashboards generate the most queries?
sql:
SELECT
  query_source.dashboard_id,
  COUNT(*) AS query_count,
  COUNT(DISTINCT executed_by) AS unique_users,
  ROUND(AVG(total_duration_ms) / 1000, 2) AS avg_duration_sec,
  SUM(read_bytes) / (1024*1024*1024) AS total_read_gb
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND query_source.dashboard_id IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY query_source.dashboard_id
ORDER BY query_count DESC
LIMIT 10

question: Group by hour of day for capacity planning
sql:
SELECT
  HOUR(start_time) AS hour_of_day,
  COUNT(*) AS query_count,
  ROUND(AVG(total_duration_ms) / 1000, 2) AS avg_sec
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
GROUP BY HOUR(start_time)
ORDER BY hour_of_day

question: Filter by time range — show query counts for different periods
sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) AS total_queries,
  SUM(CASE WHEN start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 1 DAY THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS last_1_day,
  SUM(CASE WHEN start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS last_7_days,
  SUM(CASE WHEN start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 30 DAYS THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS last_30_days
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 30 DAYS

question: Find duplicate or repeated queries
sql:
SELECT
  statement_text,
  COUNT(*) AS times_run,
  COUNT(DISTINCT executed_by) AS unique_users,
  ROUND(AVG(total_duration_ms) / 1000, 2) AS avg_sec,
  SUM(CASE WHEN from_result_cache THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS cache_hits
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND execution_status = 'FINISHED'
  AND statement_type = 'SELECT'
GROUP BY statement_text
HAVING COUNT(*) > 5
ORDER BY times_run DESC
LIMIT 20

question: Which queries read the most data?
sql:
SELECT
  executed_by,
  statement_text,
  read_bytes / (1024*1024*1024) AS read_gb,
  read_rows,
  read_files,
  read_partitions,
  total_duration_ms / 1000 AS duration_sec
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND execution_status = 'FINISHED'
ORDER BY read_bytes DESC
LIMIT 10

question: Show queries from notebooks vs dashboards vs jobs in the last 7 days
sql:
SELECT
  CASE
    WHEN query_source.notebook_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Notebook'
    WHEN query_source.dashboard_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Dashboard'
    WHEN query_source.job_info.job_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Job'
    WHEN query_source.alert_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Alert'
    WHEN query_source.pipeline_info.pipeline_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'Pipeline'
    ELSE 'Other'
  END AS source_type,
  COUNT(*) AS query_count,
  COUNT(DISTINCT executed_by) AS unique_users,
  ROUND(try_divide(AVG(total_duration_ms), 1000), 2) AS avg_duration_sec
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
GROUP BY source_type
ORDER BY query_count DESC

question: Convert milliseconds to seconds and minutes
sql:
SELECT
  total_duration_ms / 1000 AS duration_sec,
  total_duration_ms / 60000 AS duration_min,
  waiting_for_compute_duration_ms / 1000 AS compute_wait_sec,
  waiting_at_capacity_duration_ms / 1000 AS capacity_wait_sec,
  execution_duration_ms / 1000 AS exec_sec,
  compilation_duration_ms / 1000 AS compile_sec
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
LIMIT 10

question: Common client_application values and what they map to
sql:
SELECT
  client_application,
  COUNT(*) AS query_count
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND client_application IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY client_application
ORDER BY query_count DESC
LIMIT 30

question: Filter queries by a specific client_application using ILIKE pattern matching
sql:
SELECT
  client_application,
  executed_by,
  statement_text,
  try_divide(total_duration_ms, 1000) AS duration_sec
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND client_application ILIKE '%Databricks%'
ORDER BY total_duration_ms DESC
LIMIT 20

question: Which queries are waiting the longest for compute to start?
sql:
SELECT
  executed_by,
  compute.warehouse_id,
  statement_text,
  waiting_for_compute_duration_ms / 1000 AS compute_wait_sec,
  waiting_at_capacity_duration_ms / 1000 AS capacity_wait_sec,
  total_duration_ms / 1000 AS total_sec
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND (
    waiting_for_compute_duration_ms > 30000
    OR waiting_at_capacity_duration_ms > 30000
  )
ORDER BY
  waiting_for_compute_duration_ms + waiting_at_capacity_duration_ms DESC
LIMIT 20

question: Compare query volume and avg duration across BI tools (Tableau, Power BI, dbt, SQL Editor)
sql:
SELECT
  client_application,
  COUNT(*) AS query_count,
  COUNT(DISTINCT executed_by) AS unique_users,
  ROUND(try_divide(AVG(total_duration_ms), 1000), 2) AS avg_duration_sec,
  try_divide(SUM(read_bytes), (1024*1024*1024)) AS total_read_gb
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND client_application IN (
    'Tableau',
    'Power BI',
    'dbt',
    'Databricks SQL Editor'
  )
GROUP BY client_application
ORDER BY query_count DESC

question: Access struct columns (compute, query_source)
sql:
SELECT
  compute.warehouse_id,
  compute.type AS compute_type,
  query_source.dashboard_id,
  query_source.legacy_dashboard_id,
  query_source.notebook_id,
  query_source.alert_id,
  query_source.sql_query_id,
  query_source.genie_space_id,
  query_source.job_info.job_id,
  query_source.job_info.job_run_id,
  query_source.pipeline_info.pipeline_id,
  query_source.pipeline_info.update_id
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
LIMIT 10

question: Count with conditional aggregation (CASE WHEN)
sql:
SELECT
  DATE(start_time) AS query_date,
  COUNT(*) AS total,
  SUM(CASE WHEN execution_status = 'FINISHED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS succeeded,
  SUM(CASE WHEN execution_status = 'FAILED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS failed,
  SUM(CASE WHEN execution_status = 'CANCELED' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS canceled,
  SUM(CASE WHEN from_result_cache THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS from_cache,
  SUM(CASE WHEN spilled_local_bytes > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS spilled
FROM system.query.history
WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
GROUP BY DATE(start_time)
ORDER BY query_date
history.query_source.genie_space_id = genie_space_current.space_id
--rt=FROM_RELATIONSHIP_TYPE_MANY_TO_ONE--
history.query_source.dashboard_id = lakeview_dashboard_denormalized.dashboard_id
--rt=FROM_RELATIONSHIP_TYPE_MANY_TO_ONE--
history.compute.warehouse_id = warehouse_current.id
--rt=FROM_RELATIONSHIP_TYPE_MANY_TO_ONE--

question: Which users run the most queries? Show top 10.

content: SELECT executed_by, COUNT(*) AS query_count FROM system.query.history WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS GROUP BY executed_by ORDER BY query_count DESC LIMIT 10

question: Which dashboards have the slowest queries?

content: SELECT d.display_name AS dashboard_name, d.owner, d.count_queries_l30d, ROUND(try_divide(d.total_task_duration_l30d, d.count_queries_l30d) / 1000, 2) AS avg_query_sec FROM {{CATALOG}}.{{SCHEMA}}.lakeview_dashboard_denormalized d WHERE d.count_queries_l30d > 0 ORDER BY avg_query_sec DESC LIMIT 10

question: How many queries ran yesterday?

content: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_queries FROM system.query.history WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 1 DAY AND start_time < current_date()

question: List all warehouses

content: SELECT name AS warehouse_name, id AS warehouse_id, state, warehouse_type, cluster_size, auto_stop_mins, enable_serverless_compute, creator_name FROM {{CATALOG}}.{{SCHEMA}}.warehouse_current ORDER BY name

question: What is the daily query count trend over the last 30 days?

content: SELECT DATE(start_time) AS query_date, COUNT(*) AS total_queries FROM system.query.history WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 30 DAYS GROUP BY DATE(start_time) ORDER BY query_date

question: Which dashboards by name generate the most queries?

content: SELECT d.display_name AS dashboard_name, d.owner, COUNT(*) AS query_count, COUNT(DISTINCT h.executed_by) AS unique_users FROM system.query.history h JOIN {{CATALOG}}.{{SCHEMA}}.lakeview_dashboard_denormalized d ON h.query_source.dashboard_id = d.dashboard_id WHERE h.start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS GROUP BY d.display_name, d.owner ORDER BY query_count DESC LIMIT 10

question: Which dashboards have schedules?

content: SELECT display_name AS dashboard_name, owner, schedule_count, schedule_frequency_daily, schedule_frequency_hourly, schedule_frequency_weekly, count_queries_l30d FROM {{CATALOG}}.{{SCHEMA}}.lakeview_dashboard_denormalized WHERE schedule_count > 0 ORDER BY schedule_count DESC LIMIT 20

question: Dashboard usage summary

content: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_dashboards, SUM(CASE WHEN lifecycle_state = 'ACTIVE' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS active, SUM(CASE WHEN count_queries_l30d = 0 AND coalesce(views.published_l30d, 0) = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS unused_l30d, SUM(CASE WHEN count_queries_l30d > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS queried_l30d, SUM(CASE WHEN schedule_count > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS scheduled FROM {{CATALOG}}.{{SCHEMA}}.lakeview_dashboard_denormalized

question: What are the top 10 unused dashboards?

content: SELECT display_name AS dashboard_name, owner, path, count_queries_l30d, views.published_l30d, views.draft_l30d FROM {{CATALOG}}.{{SCHEMA}}.lakeview_dashboard_denormalized WHERE count_queries_l30d = 0 AND coalesce(views.published_l30d, 0) = 0 AND coalesce(views.draft_l30d, 0) = 0 AND lifecycle_state = 'ACTIVE' ORDER BY create_time LIMIT 10

question: Which warehouses by name have the most queries?

content: SELECT w.name AS warehouse_name, w.warehouse_type, w.cluster_size, COUNT(*) AS query_count, COUNT(DISTINCT h.executed_by) AS unique_users FROM system.query.history h JOIN {{CATALOG}}.{{SCHEMA}}.warehouse_current w ON h.compute.warehouse_id = w.id WHERE h.start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS GROUP BY w.name, w.warehouse_type, w.cluster_size ORDER BY query_count DESC LIMIT 10

question: Which Genie spaces are the most active?

content: SELECT g.title AS genie_space_name, g.table_count, COUNT(*) AS query_count, COUNT(DISTINCT h.executed_by) AS unique_users FROM system.query.history h JOIN {{CATALOG}}.{{SCHEMA}}.genie_space_current g ON h.query_source.genie_space_id = g.space_id WHERE h.start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 7 DAYS GROUP BY g.title, g.table_count ORDER BY query_count DESC LIMIT 10

question: List all Genie spaces

content: SELECT title AS genie_space_name, space_id, warehouse_id, table_count, instruction_count, sample_question_count, curated_question_count FROM {{CATALOG}}.{{SCHEMA}}.genie_space_current ORDER BY title

question: What are Genie spaces that have fallen off and people don't use anymore?

content:
SELECT g.title AS genie_space_name,
  g.table_count,
  g.curated_question_count,
  g.create_time,
  g.update_time,
  coalesce(recent.query_count, 0) AS queries_last_30d,
  coalesce(recent.unique_users, 0) AS unique_users_last_30d,
  coalesce(older.query_count, 0) AS queries_30_to_90d,
  coalesce(older.unique_users, 0) AS unique_users_30_to_90d
FROM {{CATALOG}}.{{SCHEMA}}.genie_space_current g
LEFT JOIN (
  SELECT query_source.genie_space_id, COUNT(*) AS query_count, COUNT(DISTINCT executed_by) AS unique_users
  FROM system.query.history
  WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 30 DAYS AND query_source.genie_space_id IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY query_source.genie_space_id
) recent ON g.space_id = recent.genie_space_id
LEFT JOIN (
  SELECT query_source.genie_space_id, COUNT(*) AS query_count, COUNT(DISTINCT executed_by) AS unique_users
  FROM system.query.history
  WHERE start_time >= current_date() - INTERVAL 90 DAYS AND start_time < current_date() - INTERVAL 30 DAYS AND query_source.genie_space_id IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY query_source.genie_space_id
) older ON g.space_id = older.genie_space_id
WHERE coalesce(recent.query_count, 0) = 0
  AND coalesce(older.query_count, 0) > 0
ORDER BY older.query_count DESC
LIMIT 20

question: Find me the most expensive dashboards with low viewers

content:
SELECT d.display_name AS dashboard_name,
  d.owner,
  d.count_queries_l30d,
  ROUND(d.total_task_duration_l30d / 1000, 2) AS total_duration_sec_l30d,
  coalesce(d.views.published_l30d, 0) AS published_views_l30d,
  coalesce(d.views.published_users_l30d, 0) AS unique_viewers_l30d,
  coalesce(d.views.draft_l30d, 0) AS draft_views_l30d,
  d.schedule_count
FROM {{CATALOG}}.{{SCHEMA}}.lakeview_dashboard_denormalized d
WHERE d.count_queries_l30d > 0
  AND coalesce(d.views.published_users_l30d, 0) <= 2
ORDER BY d.total_task_duration_l30d DESC
LIMIT 20

question: Which 10 dashboards cost the most over the configured cost window?

content: SELECT dashboard_name, owner, statements, ROUND(cost_usd, 2) AS cost_usd FROM {{CATALOG}}.{{SCHEMA}}.lakeview_dashboard_cost ORDER BY cost_usd DESC LIMIT 10

question: Which 10 Genie spaces cost the most over the configured cost window?

content: SELECT genie_space_title, statements, ROUND(cost_usd, 2) AS cost_usd, unique_users FROM {{CATALOG}}.{{SCHEMA}}.genie_space_cost ORDER BY cost_usd DESC LIMIT 10

question: How much did the dashboard called 'Sales Overview' cost over the configured cost window?

content: SELECT dashboard_name, owner, statements, ROUND(cost_usd, 2) AS cost_usd, ROUND(dbus, 4) AS dbus FROM {{CATALOG}}.{{SCHEMA}}.lakeview_dashboard_cost WHERE dashboard_name ILIKE '%Sales Overview%'

question: What is the total dashboard-driven cost over the configured cost window?

content: SELECT ROUND(SUM(cost_usd), 2) AS total_dashboard_cost_usd, SUM(statements) AS total_statements FROM {{CATALOG}}.{{SCHEMA}}.lakeview_dashboard_cost

question: What is the total Genie-driven cost over the configured cost window?

content: SELECT ROUND(SUM(cost_usd), 2) AS total_genie_cost_usd, SUM(statements) AS total_statements FROM {{CATALOG}}.{{SCHEMA}}.genie_space_cost

question: Which user runs the most expensive queries? Show top 10.

content: SELECT executed_by, COUNT(*) AS statements, ROUND(SUM(attributed_cost_usd), 2) AS cost_usd FROM {{CATALOG}}.{{SCHEMA}}.query_cost_attribution GROUP BY executed_by ORDER BY cost_usd DESC LIMIT 10

question: Show daily cost trend over the last 30 days, broken out by source type (dashboard, genie, notebook, other).

content: SELECT date_trunc('DAY', start_time) AS day, CASE WHEN dashboard_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'dashboard' WHEN genie_space_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'genie_space' WHEN notebook_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'notebook' WHEN job_id IS NOT NULL THEN 'job' ELSE 'other' END AS source_type, ROUND(SUM(attributed_cost_usd), 2) AS cost_usd FROM {{CATALOG}}.{{SCHEMA}}.query_cost_attribution GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1, 2

question: Which warehouse drives the most cost?

content: SELECT warehouse_name, cluster_size, statements, ROUND(cost_usd, 2) AS cost_usd, dashboards_touched, genie_spaces_touched FROM {{CATALOG}}.{{SCHEMA}}.warehouse_cost ORDER BY cost_usd DESC LIMIT 10

question: What is the average cost per statement for each dashboard?

content: SELECT dashboard_name, statements, ROUND(avg_cost_per_statement_usd, 4) AS avg_cost_per_statement_usd, ROUND(cost_usd, 2) AS total_cost_usd FROM {{CATALOG}}.{{SCHEMA}}.lakeview_dashboard_cost WHERE statements > 0 ORDER BY avg_cost_per_statement_usd DESC LIMIT 20

question: Which Genie spaces are used by the most distinct people, and what is the cost?

content: SELECT genie_space_title, unique_users, statements, ROUND(cost_usd, 2) AS cost_usd FROM {{CATALOG}}.{{SCHEMA}}.genie_space_cost ORDER BY unique_users DESC LIMIT 10

title: Query Insights — Cost, Performance, Inventory
description: Ask anything about DBSQL workloads on this workspace: how much each dashboard, Genie space, or user is costing; which queries are slow, queued, or spilling; how much of your spend comes from BI tools vs ad-hoc; how usage has trended over time. Grounded in system.query.history, system.billing.* (with account_prices/list_prices fallback), and a workspace-inventory pipeline that resolves dashboard, Genie space, and warehouse IDs to human names.
